column1: Applied after the Prescription Grid lookup, in ascending numeric priority (the rule_id prefix is a label, priority is the sort key). HARD_SAFETY rules can block or rewrite a session and are never overridden by any goal, preference or prompt. PROGRAM_DEFAULT and FATIGUE_ADJUSTMENT rules are coaching defaults with named exemptions.

column6: Do not serve. Walk the Substitutions list in priority order and return the first entry compatible with the user's skill level, equipment, restrictions and exclusion history.
column7: Advanced lifts are never given to a beginner. The app walks the swap list until it finds one the user can actually and safely do.

column6: Force rir_min to 2 or higher. End set if movement speed or landing quality drops.
column7: Explosive lifts are never taken to failure. A failed jump squat is a fall.

column6: Force rir_min to the exercise Min RIR or higher.
column7: Free weight compounds, Power lifts and Advanced lifts always stop short of failure. Nobody fails a squat alone in a university gym.

column6: Clamp reps_min to 4 and pct_1rm_max to 88.
column7: The app never prescribes a near maximal single, double or triple. That needs a coach and a spotter in the room.

column5: user has not completed pre exercise screening
column6: Block program generation. Run the screening flow. Route on the outcome per the Screening Outcomes sheet.
column7: Everybody is screened before a single set is prescribed, and the screening outcome decides what happens next. Screening alone does not guarantee safety.

column5: stop symptom reported: chest pain or discomfort, dizziness, fainting, unusual shortness of breath, palpitations, numbness or tingling
column6: End the session immediately. Show the stop message. Do not generate further sessions until the user confirms symptoms resolved, or professional clearance where the Screening Outcomes sheet requires it.
column7: Symptoms are never trained through. Chest pain, dizziness and fainting also prompt the user to seek medical advice. Acute sharp joint pain instead ends that exercise and routes to a pain swap (SW02) plus the Injury Modifications region rules, without freezing the whole program.

column6: Cap load increase at 5 percent per week per exercise, or 2.5kg upper and 5kg lower body, whichever is smaller.
column7: Slow progression is the safe kind. Big jumps are how people get hurt.

column5: required equipment not available to the user
column6: Walk the Substitutions list and return the first compatible entry.
column7: The app never prescribes gear the user does not have.

column6: Serve the highest priority substitute that does not need a spotter, or attach the no spotter safety cue (no collars on bench, safeties set in the rack).
column7: Barbell bench and back squat variations get a spotter or a safe setup, never neither.

column6: Force rir_min to 2. Block Advanced and Power classes. Start every load light, around RIR 4 to 5 in week one, and work up over two sessions.
column7: New lifters build the movement first, then the effort, then the load.

column5: rest below 45 seconds outside Fat Loss circuits and Interval work
column7: A default, not a law. Fat Loss circuits and Intervals are exempt because short rest is the point there.
Past a minute the hold trains endurance, not the target muscle. A preference, not a safety fact.

column5: 6 completed training weeks, OR 2 consecutive weeks of stalled progression, rising logged RPE, or persistent soreness
column6: Offer a deload week (cut sets 40 percent, hold load, raise rir_min by 1). At 6 weeks it is a prompt the user can accept or defer once; on fatigue signals it is applied.
column7: A planned easy week, offered not forced at the 6 week mark, applied when the body actually shows fatigue. Recovery matters but rigid rules hurt adherence.

column5: user logs RPE 10 or failure on 3 or more sets in a session
column6: Reduce next session sets by 1 and raise rir_min by 1.
column7: Pull the user back before they dig a hole.

column5: user reports poor sleep or a missed session
column6: Raise rir_min by 1 for that session.
column7: An easier session done beats a hard session skipped.
